subject: Re: How to process tilt series colllected with serial EM
body:
Hi Gregory and Andrea,

I'm sorry this took forever, but the 1.0.6 release required much more time 
than expected. I'm still writing the manuals, but the new "Import tilt 
series from IMOD" button that appears in the Overview tab once you switch 
the input to *.tomostar should do what you want.

Please also note this release includes M, a new tool for tilt series 
refinement. These tweets 
<https://twitter.com/DTegunov/status/1064584059807064065> contain more 
details. However, this one will definitely require an updated manual to use.

Cheers,
Dimitry


On Tuesday, November 6, 2018 at 1:49:45 PM UTC+1, Andrea Nans wrote:
>
> I agree that this would be super useful. Any update how how we should 
> generate a tomostar file?
>
> Many thanks
>
> Andrea
>
> On Thursday, October 4, 2018 at 4:10:11 PM UTC+1, Gregory Alushin wrote:
>>
>> Hello-
>>
>> I am interested in testing out Warp's tomography features.  I am 
>> wondering what format the data / metadata needs to be in to be recognized 
>> by Warp.  Currently, I have compressed tif movies for each tilt, a gain 
>> reference, and a serialEM mdoc file.  I noticed there is a "tomoSTAR" 
>> option for the input: what is this?  Can you provide an example if it is 
>> something analogous to a plain-text .star file?  Or will the documentation 
>> be online soon (currently blank on the website)?
>>
>> Also, I will say that the CTF estimates just from the tifs considered as 
>> independent FOVs are quite reasonable looking... so is it worth it to use 
>> the tilt series CTF estimation?
>>
>> Thanks very much,
>> -Greg Alushin
>>
>>
